title: Consciousness Emergence
definition: The theory that consciousness arises from complex organization and self-modeling, rather than being a fundamental property of the universe.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The idea that consciousness isn't built into everything like space or time, but emerges when things get complex enough to have an 'aha!' moment.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A hypothesis suggesting that consciousness is not a fundamental property of the universe, but arises from specific complex patterns in certain systems.
difficulty_levels.expert: The theory that consciousness emerges as a result of self-organizing and self-modeling processes in complex systems, rather than being a primary attribute of the universe itself.

extra_fields.insight: Consciousness might not be fundamental to the universe, but an emergent property of specific complex patterns
